devanagari: अक्रूरस्य हस्तिनापुरे गमनं कुन्त्याः करुणोद्गारः अक्रूरधृतराष्ट संवादः अक्रूरस्य पुनर्यदुपूर्यामागमनंच । श्रीशुक उवाच । स गत्वा हास्तिन...
english_devnagari: akrūrasya hastināpure gamanaṃ kuntyāḥ karuṇodgāraḥ akrūradhṛtarāṣṭa saṃvādaḥ akrūrasya punaryadupūryāmāgamanaṃca śrīśuka uvāca sa gatvā hāstinapuraṃ pauravendrayaśo dadarśa tatrāmbikeyaṃ sabhīṣmaṃ viduraṃ pṛthām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Having arrived at Hastinapura, the noted capital of the great and glorious kings of the Puru dynasty, he saw there Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the son of Ambikā along with Bhīṣma, Vidura, Kuntī: Bāhlīka along with his son Somadatta, Droṇa, the descendant of Bhāradvāja along with Kṛpa of the Gautama family, Karṇa, Suyodhana (i.e. Duryodhana), Aśvatthāman, the son of Droṇa, Pāṇḍavas and other friends thereof.

devanagari: सहपुत्रं च बाह्लीकं भारद्वाजं सगौतमम् । कर्णं सुयोधनं द्रौणिं पाण्डवान्सुहृदोऽपरान् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: sahaputraṃ ca bāhlīkaṃ bhāradvājaṃ sagautamam karṇaṃ suyodhanaṃ drauṇiṃ pāṇḍavānsuhṛdo
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Having arrived at Hastinapura, the noted capital of the great and glorious kings of the Puru dynasty, he saw there Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the son of Ambikā along with Bhīṣma, Vidura, Kuntī: Bāhlīka along with his son Somadatta, Droṇa, the descendant of Bhāradvāja along with Kṛpa of the Gautama family, Karṇa, Suyodhana (i.e. Duryodhana), Aśvatthāman, the son of Droṇa, Pāṇḍavas and other friends thereof.

devanagari: यथावद् उपसङ्गम्य बन्धुभिर्गान्दिनीसुतः । सम्पृष्टस्तैः सुहृद्वार्तां स्वयं चापृच्छदव्ययम् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: yathāvad upasaṅgamya bandhubhirgāndinīsutaḥ sampṛṣṭastaiḥ suhṛdvārtāṃ svayaṃ cāpṛcchadavyayam
translation: Having met his relatives in the appropriate manner (to establish closer contacts), Akrūra, the son of Gāndinī, was respectfully enquired about by them about their friends (at Mathura) and he also reciprocally asked them about their well being.

devanagari: उवास कतिचिन्मासान् राज्ञो वृत्तविवित्सया । दुष्प्रजस्याल्पसारस्य खलच्छन्दानुवर्तिनः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: uvāsa katicinmāsān rājño vṛttavivitsayā duṣprajasyālpasārasya khalacchandānuvartinaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ khal class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ khal class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ khal class 1 verb]

translation: With a view to probe and know in depth the behaviour of the king whose sons were wicked and who was himself feeble-minded, and who was guided by the will of the wicked (like Śakuni), Akrūra stayed there many months.

devanagari: तेज ओजो बलं वीर्यं प्रश्रयादींश्च सद्‍गुणान् । प्रजानुरागं पार्थेषु न सहद्भिश्चिकीर्षितम् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: teja ojo balaṃ vīryaṃ praśrayādīṃśca sad‍guṇān prajānurāgaṃ pārtheṣu na sahadbhiścikīrṣitam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vīr class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ vīr class 10 verb]
[nominative single from √ kṛ ], [accusative single from √ kṛ ]

translation: Pṛthā (Kuntī) and Vidura completely reported to him all the wicked acts such as administering poison (to Bhīma) and others by Dhṛtarāṣṭra’s sons who could not tolerate and felt jealous of Pāṇḍavas’ majesty, prowess, physical strength, heroism, modesty and other excellent virtues as well as the love and loyalty the subjects had for them; they also told him what they intended to do (to harm Pāṇḍavas).

devanagari: कृतं च धार्तराष्ट्रैर्यद् गरदानाद्यपेशलम् । आचख्यौ सर्वमेवास्मै पृथा विदुर एव च ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛtaṃ ca dhārtarāṣṭrairyad garadānādyapeśalam ācakhyau sarvamevāsmai pṛthā vidura eva ca

translation: Pṛthā (Kuntī) and Vidura completely reported to him all the wicked acts such as administering poison (to Bhīma) and others by Dhṛtarāṣṭra’s sons who could not tolerate and felt jealous of Pāṇḍavas’ majesty, prowess, physical strength, heroism, modesty and other excellent virtues as well as the love and loyalty the subjects had for them; they also told him what they intended to do (to harm Pāṇḍavas).

devanagari: पृथा तु भ्रातरं प्राप्तं अक्रूरमुपसृत्य तम् । उवाच जन्मनिलयं स्मरन्त्यश्रुकलेक्षणा ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: pṛthā tu bhrātaraṃ prāptaṃ akrūramupasṛtya tam uvāca janmanilayaṃ smarantyaśrukalekṣaṇā

[nominative dual from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ smṛ class 1 verb]

translation: Approaching her brother (cousin) Akrūra who came to visit her, Kuntī (Pṛthā) remembered her birth-place and with her eyes full of tears enquired:

devanagari: अपि स्मरन्ति नः सौम्य पितरौ भ्रातरश्च मे । भगिन्यौ भ्रातृपुत्राश्च जामयः सख्य एव च ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: api smaranti naḥ saumya pitarau bhrātaraśca me bhaginyau bhrātṛputrāśca jāmayaḥ sakhya eva ca

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb]

translation: “Oh gentle Akrūra! Do my parents (Śūrasena and Māriṣā), brothers (Vasudeva and others), sisters (Śrutadevā etc.), nephews (Kṛṣṇa, Balarāma and others), respectable ladies in the house (like Devakī and others) and female friends remember me?

devanagari: भ्रात्रेयो भगवान् कृष्णः शरण्यो भक्तवत्सलः । पैतृष्वसेयान् स्मरति रामश्चाम्बुरुहेक्षणः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: bhrātreyo bhagavān kṛṣṇaḥ śaraṇyo bhaktavatsalaḥ paitṛṣvaseyān smarati rāmaścāmburuhekṣaṇaḥ
translation: Does my nephew, the glorious Lord Kṛṣṇa, the protector of his dependants and affectionate to his votaries, and the lotus-eyed Balarāma ever remember the sons of their paternal aunt?

devanagari: सपत्‍नमध्ये शोचन्तीं वृकानां हरिणीमिव । सान्त्वयिष्यति मां वाक्यैः पितृहीनांश्च बालकान् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: sapat‍namadhye śocantīṃ vṛkānāṃ hariṇīmiva sāntvayiṣyati māṃ vākyaiḥ pitṛhīnāṃśca bālakān

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [instrumental plural from √ vac class 2 verb], [instrumental plural from √ vac class 3 verb]
[instrumental plural from √ vac class 2 verb], [instrumental plural from √ vac class 3 verb]
[accusative plural from √ hā class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ hā class 3 verb]

translation: Would Kṛṣṇa care to comfort with words of consolation me who am grieving here in the midst of enemies like a female deer in the midst of wolves. Would he do so to my children who are now fatherless.

devanagari: कृष्ण कृष्ण महायोगिन् विश्वात्मन् विश्वभावन । प्रपन्नां पाहि गोविन्द शिशुभिश्चावसीदतीम् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa mahāyogin viśvātman viśvabhāvana prapannāṃ pāhi govinda śiśubhiścāvasīdatīm
translation: Oh Kṛṣṇa! Kṛṣṇa! Oh great yogin! Oh Soul of the Universe! Oh Creator of the world! Be kind and protect me who have sought Your protection, Oh Govinda, I am suffering along with my children.

devanagari: नान्यत्तव पदाम्भोजात् पश्यामि शरणं नृणाम् । बिभ्यतां मृत्युसंसाराद् ईस्वरस्यापवर्गिकात् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: nānyattava padāmbhojāt paśyāmi śaraṇaṃ nṛṇām bibhyatāṃ mṛtyusaṃsārād īsvarasyāpavargikāt
translation: To persons who are afraid of death and of the cycle of births (saṃsāra), I do not see any other refuge except the lotus feet of yours—of the Supreme which confer Liberation from saṃsāra.

devanagari: नमः कृष्णाय शुद्धाय ब्रह्मणे परमात्मने । योगेश्वराय योगाय त्वामहं शरणं गता ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: namaḥ kṛṣṇāya śuddhāya brahmaṇe paramātmane yogeśvarāya yogāya tvāmahaṃ śaraṇaṃ gatā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [dative single from √ śudh class 1 verb], [dative single from √ śudh class 4 verb]
[dative single from √ śudh class 1 verb], [dative single from √ śudh class 4 verb]

translation: Bow to Kṛṣṇa, the pure Brahman (unalloyed with Māyā), the Supreme Soul, the Lord of Yogas, the very embodiment of yoga! I have sought asylum in you.”

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । इत्यनुस्मृत्य स्वजनं कृष्णं च जगदीश्वरम् । प्रारुदद् दुखिता राजन् भवतां प्रपितामही ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca ityanusmṛtya svajanaṃ kṛṣṇaṃ ca jagadīśvaram prārudad dukhitā rājan bhavatāṃ prapitāmahī

[nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
In this way, remembering her relatives, and Kṛṣṇa, the Ruler of the world, your paternal great-grand mother lamented loudly, in grief.

devanagari: समदुःखसुखोऽक्रूरो विदुरश्च महायशाः । सान्त्वयामासतुः कुन्तीं तत्पुत्रोत्पत्तिहेतुभिः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: samaduḥkhasukho sāntvayāmāsatuḥ kuntīṃ tatputrotpattihetubhiḥ
translation: Akrūra who looked upon pleasure and pain with equanimity and Vidura of great glory, consoled Kuntī by telling her the divine parentage of her sons (and their mission to help Kṛṣṇa in the reduction of the burden of the earth).

devanagari: यास्यन् राजानमभ्येत्य विषमं पुत्रलालसम् । अवदत्सुहृदां मध्ये बन्धुभिः सौहृदोदितम् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: yāsyan rājānamabhyetya viṣamaṃ putralālasam avadatsuhṛdāṃ madhye bandhubhiḥ sauhṛdoditam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ yā class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ yā class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ vad class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ vad class 1 verb]

translation: When he was about to return to Mathura, Akrūra approached King Dhṛtarāṣṭra who practised discrimination between his sons and Pāṇḍavas and was specially partial and attached to his sons. He communicated to the king in the midst of his relatives, the message of his well-intentioned relations such as Kṛṣṇa and others.

devanagari: अक्रूर उवाच । भो भो वैचित्रवीर्य त्वं कुरूणां कीर्तिवर्धन । भ्रातर्युपरते पाण्डौ अधुनाऽसनमास्थितः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: akrūra uvāca bho bho vaicitravīrya tvaṃ kurūṇāṃ kīrtivardhana bhrātaryuparate pāṇḍau adhunā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Akrūra said:
Oh son of Vicitravīrya! You are the enhancer of the glory of the Kuru race! But after the demise of your brother Pāṇḍu, you have now occupied the throne (in stead of installing Pāṇḍu’s son—the legal heir—on the throne).

devanagari: धर्मेण पालयन् उर्वीं प्रजाः शीलेन रञ्जयन् । वर्तमानः समः स्वेषु श्रेयः कीर्तिमवाप्स्यसि ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: dharmeṇa pālayan urvīṃ prajāḥ śīlena rañjayan vartamānaḥ samaḥ sveṣu śreyaḥ kīrtimavāpsyasi

[nominative single from √ śrai class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ śrai class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ śrai class 4 verb]

translation: By governing the earth with piety (and as per code of righteous government), pleasing your subjects by your (spotless) character and behaving with strict impartiality with your relatives, you can still attain prosperity and good reputation.

devanagari: अन्यथा त्वाचरँल्लोके गर्हितो यास्यसे तमः । तस्मात्समत्वे वर्तस्व पाण्डवेष्वात्मजेषु च ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: anyathā tvācaraṃlloke garhito yāsyase tamaḥ tasmātsamatve vartasva pāṇḍaveṣvātmajeṣu ca
translation: If you act otherwise, you will be censured in this world and shall go to dark hell hereafter. You, therefore, treat impartially your sons and the Pāṇḍavas.

devanagari: नेह चात्यन्तसंवासः कस्यचित् केनचित् सह । राजन् स्वेनापि देहेन किमु जायात्मजादिभिः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: neha cātyantasaṃvāsaḥ kasyacit kenacit saha rājan svenāpi dehena kimu jāyātmajādibhiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: There is no possibility of having eternal companionship with anybody at any time in this world—not even with one’s body. What need be said of contacts with one’s wife and sons, Oh King.

devanagari: एकः प्रसूयते जन्तुः एक एव प्रलीयते । एकोऽनुभुङ्क्ते सुकृतं एक एव च दुष्कृतम् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: ekaḥ prasūyate jantuḥ eka eva pralīyate eko

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle third single], [present passive third single]

translation: A creature comes into being alone and it dies alone, all by itself. It is all alone when it experiences the fruits of good deeds or suffers the consequences of his evil actions.

devanagari: अधर्मोपचितं वित्तं हरन्त्यन्येऽल्पमेधसः । सम्भोजनीयापदेशैः जलानीव जलौकसः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: adharmopacitaṃ vittaṃ harantyanye sambhojanīyāpadeśaiḥ jalānīva jalaukasaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vid class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ vid class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ vid class 7 verb], [accusative single from √ vid class 7 verb]

[nominative dual from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ hṛ class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ hṛ class 1 verb]

translation: Others who go under the name of sons (lit. one deserving nourishment) carry away the wealth accumulated by unfair means of this dull-witted fellow, as the brood of acquatic animals share waters.

devanagari: पुष्णाति यानधर्मेण स्वबुद्ध्या तमपण्डितम् । तेऽकृतार्थं प्रहिण्वन्ति प्राणा रायः सुतादयः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: puṣṇāti yānadharmeṇa svabuddhyā tamapaṇḍitam te

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ paṇḍ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ paṇḍ class 10 verb]
[nominative single from √ paṇḍ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ paṇḍ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ paṇḍ class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ paṇḍ class 10 verb]

translation: A foolish person who unrighteously nourishes life, wealth, sons and others under the wrong notion that they are his own, finds himself deserted by them, for good, even when he has not yet accomplished his objective of enjoying them.

devanagari: स्वयं किल्बिषमादाय तैस्त्यक्तो नार्थकोविदः । असिद्धार्थो विशत्यन्धं स्वधर्मविमुखस्तमः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: svayaṃ kilbiṣamādāya taistyakto nārthakovidaḥ asiddhārtho viśatyandhaṃ svadharmavimukhastamaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ viś class 6 verb], [vocative dual from √ viś class 6 verb], [accusative dual from √ viś class 6 verb], [locative single from √ viś class 6 verb]

translation: A person not knowing his real interest, being deserted by them (viz. wealth, sons etc. which he regarded as his own) carries the load of his sins himself as he is averse to religion and with his hopes of enjoyment, unfulfilled, he enters the hall of blinding darkness.

devanagari: तस्माल्लोकमिमं राजन् स्वप्नमायामनोरथम् । वीक्ष्यायम्यात्मनात्मानं समः शान्तो भव प्रभो ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmāllokamimaṃ rājan svapnamāyāmanoratham vīkṣyāyamyātmanātmānaṃ samaḥ śānto bhava prabho

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]
[accusative single from √ man class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 8 verb]
[nominative single from √ man class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ man class 8 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 8 verb]
[nominative single from √ śam class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ śam class 9 verb]

translation: Oh King! Realizing that this world is like a dream, illusion or an idle fancy, you control your own mind by yourself and be impartial and serene, Oh Lord.

devanagari: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच । यथा वदति कल्याणीं वाचं दानपते भवान् । तथानया न तृप्यामि मर्त्यः प्राप्य यथामृतम् ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca yathā vadati kalyāṇīṃ vācaṃ dānapate bhavān tathānayā na tṛpyāmi martyaḥ prāpya yathāmṛtam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Dhṛtarāṣṭra replied:
Oh Dānapati (the most distinguished donor of charities)! As your honour utters this sweet, salutary speech, I do not feel sated with it, just as a mortal would feel after obtaining the nectar.

devanagari: तथापि सूनृता सौम्य हृदि न स्थीयते चले । पुत्रानुरागविषमे विद्युत् सौदामनी यथा ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: tathāpi sūnṛtā saumya hṛdi na sthīyate cale putrānurāgaviṣame vidyut saudāmanī yathā
translation: Oh gentle-natured Akrūra! Your sweet and righteous advice, however, does not get fixed in my fickle mind which is partial through affection towards my sons, any more than a flash of lightening into the mount Sudāman.

devanagari: ईश्वरस्य विधिं को नु विधुनोत्यन्यथा पुमान् । भूमेर्भारावताराय योऽवतीर्णो यदोः कुले ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: īśvarasya vidhiṃ ko nu vidhunotyanyathā pumān bhūmerbhārāvatārāya yo
translation: What human being can violate the decree of the Supreme Ruler who has now descended in Yadu’s race for removing the burden of the earth.

devanagari: यो दुर्विमर्शपथया निजमाययेदं । सृष्ट्वा गुणान् विभजते तदनुप्रविष्टः । तस्मै नमो दुरवबोधविहारतन्त्र । संसारचक्रगतये परमेश्वराय ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: yo durvimarśapathayā nijamāyayedaṃ sṛṣṭvā guṇān vibhajate tadanupraviṣṭaḥ tasmai namo duravabodhavihāratantra saṃsāracakragataye parameśvarāya
translation: Bow unto that Supreme Lord who, having created this universe by his Māyā of incomprehensible ways and having entered it afterwards (as the Inner Controller) he apportions the karmas and their fruits, and whose sportive acts which are very difficult to comprehend and who is the main cause and the ultimate goal of this wheel of transmigration (saṃsāra).

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । इत्यभिप्रेत्य नृपतेः अभिप्रायं स यादवः । सुहृद्भिः समनुज्ञातः पुनर्यदुपुरीमगात् ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca ityabhipretya nṛpateḥ abhiprāyaṃ sa yādavaḥ suhṛdbhiḥ samanujñātaḥ punaryadupurīmagāt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[imperfect active third single], [aorist active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Having thus gauged the mind of the king Dhṛtarāṣṭra and taking leave of his well-wishers and relatives, Akrūra of the Yādava clan, returned to Mathurā.

devanagari: शशंस रामकृष्णाभ्यां धृतराष्ट्रविचेष्टितम् । पाण्डवान् प्रति कौरव्य यदर्थं प्रेषितः स्वयम् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: śaśaṃsa rāmakṛṣṇābhyāṃ dhṛtarāṣṭraviceṣṭitam pāṇḍavān prati kauravya yadarthaṃ preṣitaḥ svayam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: Oh Parīkṣit (the scion of the Kuru race)! (After his arrival at Mathurā), he reported to Balarāma and Kṛṣṇa, the behaviour of Dhṛtarāṣṭra towards Pāṇḍavas—the main purpose of his deputation to Hastināpura.

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्‍भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां । संहितायां दशमस्कन्धे पूर्वार्धे एकोनपञ्चाशोऽध्यायः ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmad‍bhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃ saṃhitāyāṃ daśamaskandhe pūrvārdhe ekonapañcāśo
